What is the ultimate pass rate for French
The 2015 ultimate pass rate for the NCLEX-RN
written in French was 50%. We recognize that the
pass rate is low, and we’re working with government
and educators to help determine the cause. A rigorous
translation process is used to develop the French version
of the exam. It is conducted by Canadians using federal
translation standards, and exam items are reviewed by
a translation panel consisting of three to six Canadian
bilingual RNs. These RNs are required to be fluent in
French and English, and to be practising in a bilingual
What happens if a student fails the NCLEX-RN?
Writers who make an unsuccessful attempt on the
NCLEX-RN are provided with a Candidate’s Performance
Report (CPR). This report provides them with information
about how they did in each test section of the exam. The
After the Exam page on the NCSBN’s website includes
information about using this report to help prepare for
another writing attempt. In Ontario, there is no limit on
the number of times an applicant can attempt to write the
Is public safety affected by having no limits
on the number of times a writer can attempt
The public’s safety is not impacted by this change in the
number of exam writes. The safety and security features
built into the NCLEX-RN help to ensure that a nurse’s
ability to provide safe care is not impacted by the number
